title: feat(enter): structured 5xx error signal in logs + Tinybird
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: The hive's radar gets an upgrade
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our internal error tracking with structured logs and Tinybird integration. Now our agents can spot and squash bugs faster than ever.
gist.summary: Implemented structured 5xx error signaling in Workers Logs and added a Tinybird datasource for querying recent server errors.
gist.impact: This reduces log noise and allows our internal AI agents to quickly query and diagnose server errors, leading to faster bug resolution.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and the monitor robot looking at a clean, organized radar screen or a neat stack of glowing error logs. The Nomnom creature could be eating the 'noisy' logs while the robot holds a clean, structured glowing crystal (representing the structured error envelope).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot looking at a glowing lime green (#ecf874) radar screen showing clean, organized data points. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a messy pile of old paper logs nearby. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
